channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a wide range of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect AI agents to tools like GitHub and Notion for enhanced application building.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential in enhancing event experiences.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, emphasizing the transition to agentic AI with various technical tracks.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, enhancing GPU and NPU acceleration and supporting PyTorch and JAX, marking a significant update for deploying GenAI.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: The Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud experts aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and custom commands for tailored development experiences.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: New features for Gemini Code Assist, including Finish Changes and Outlines, aim to reduce developer friction by automating code completion and providing high-level summaries.

item.title: Here's my favorite email trick for cleaning up inbox clutter - automatically
item.description: A simple email trick can help users automatically clean up their inboxes, reducing clutter from ads and newsletters.

item.title: Apple update looks like Czech mate for locked-out iPhone user
item.description: A US university student is unable to access their iPhone due to a removed character from Apple's Czech keyboard, highlighting issues with device accessibility.

item.title: After using Lenovo's new Yoga laptop, I'm wondering if Windows makers are running out of ideas
item.description: Lenovo's Yoga 7a 2-in-1 laptop is well-built but its mid-tier hardware raises questions about innovation among Windows laptop makers.

item.title: The $30 Google TV stick may be the budget Chromecast successor we've been waiting for
item.description: Walmart is set to release a new $30 Google TV stick, which is anticipated to be a budget-friendly successor to Chromecast, appealing to streaming enthusiasts.

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman addressed concerns raised in a New Yorker article and responded to an attack on his home, emphasizing transparency and trust.

item.title: How Salesforce and ServiceNow are squaring off in the battle for the helpdesk
item.description: Salesforce and ServiceNow are competing fiercely in the helpdesk market, with Salesforce focusing on user engagement while ServiceNow emphasizes governance of AI agents.

item.title: I stopped guessing which AA batteries are dead - this charging station keeps them in check for me
item.description: The Ostation 2 Pro battery charger by Olight offers a solution for managing AA batteries, allowing users to easily check their charge status.

item.title: Two different attackers poisoned popular open source tools - and showed us the future of supply chain compromise
item.description: Recent supply chain attacks compromised popular open source tools, highlighting vulnerabilities that could affect thousands of organizations and stressing the need for better security measures.

item.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
item.description: A critique of the AI-generated illustration accompanying the New Yorker profile of Sam Altman, highlighting its unsettling portrayal and the implications of AI in art.

item.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
item.description: The FBI's surveillance capabilities extend to push notifications, raising concerns about privacy and security in digital communications.

item.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
item.description: A humorous exploration of a rumor regarding Mitski's father being a CIA operative, showcasing the absurdity of internet gossip.

item.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
item.description: The article discusses how the internet's evolution has outpaced our ability to discern truth, particularly with AI-generated content and restricted data.

item.title: The best AR and MR glasses in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best AR and MR glasses available in 2026, focusing on their ability to blend digital and real-world experiences for users.

item.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
item.description: The Iranian regime's state media has effectively countered the White House's online presence by showcasing real footage of the war, highlighting the stark contrast in messaging. This strategy has raised questions about the effectiveness of the U.S. government's communication efforts in times of conflict.

item.title: Hungarian government creds left in the safe hands of 'FrankLampard'
item.description: Hungary's government faced a security breach exposing nearly 800 state logins, including sensitive defense accounts, due to poor password practices. This incident underscores the vulnerabilities in national security systems and the importance of cybersecurity.

item.title: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description: GitHub Copilot is implementing new limits and retiring the Opus 4.6 Fast feature due to increased usage patterns. This move aims to manage the platform's performance and ensure a better experience for users.

item.title: Snowflake manager explains the 'Spider-Man' theory of AI agent data access
item.description: Snowflake's director discussed the importance of data quality and governance in developing AI agents, suggesting that access to clean data is a key factor in AI success. This perspective shifts the focus from just model capabilities to data management.

item.title: Here's how to watch the Artemis II splashdown
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully returned astronauts after traveling farther from Earth than any humans before. This achievement marks a significant milestone in space exploration amidst global challenges.

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description: GitHub has renamed its coding agent to Copilot cloud agent and is updating its data schema accordingly. This change reflects the evolving nature of the platform and its services.

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: Anthropic has temporarily banned the creator of OpenClaw from accessing Claude following a pricing change, indicating a tightening of access to its AI tools. This action raises questions about user management and pricing strategies.

item.title: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description: Due to increased abuse of the free trial system, GitHub is pausing new trials for Copilot Pro to maintain platform integrity. This decision aims to protect legitimate developers from disruptions.

item.title: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description: GitHub has imposed a limit of 50 reruns on Actions workflows, which will now result in a failed check suite if exceeded. This change aims to streamline workflow management.

item.title: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description: Copilot CLI activity is now included in overall usage metrics, providing a more comprehensive view of user engagement with the platform. This update reflects GitHub's commitment to transparency in usage data.

item.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
item.description: The Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver is currently available at a 50% discount, making it an affordable option for DIY enthusiasts and home repairs.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcases advancements in AI and robotics that are transforming various industries, emphasizing the importance of robot learning and simulation.

item.title: This Ryobi yard essentials bundle packs a free power tool - how to get yours
item.description: Home Depot is offering a Ryobi yard essentials bundle that includes a free power tool, promoting gardening and lawn care products.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: Anthropic's new AI model raises concerns as it could be exploited by hackers, prompting calls for improved security measures in AI development.

item.title: Microsoft's Copilot strategy is just more user abuse from Redmond, says Mozilla
item.description: Mozilla criticizes Microsoft's Copilot strategy, arguing that the company is prioritizing AI features over user choice and experience.

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: A lawsuit against OpenAI claims that ChatGPT contributed to a stalking incident by ignoring warnings about a user's dangerous behavior.

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
item.description: An individual was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, raising security concerns for tech leaders.

item.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
item.description: A 20-year-old man has been arrested for throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's house, with the incident captured on surveillance cameras.

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: A tutorial has been released for beginners on how to use the GitHub Copilot CLI, providing step-by-step guidance for new users.

item.title: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description: GitHub Copilot's cloud agent has improved its validation tools, making them 20% faster for code security and quality checks.

item.title: Project Glasswing and open source software: The good, the bad, and the ugly
item.description: Anthropic's Project Glasswing aims to allocate $100 million in AI resources to identify and fix vulnerabilities in open source software using its Mythos AI program. This initiative highlights the dual-edged nature of AI, as it can both discover and potentially exploit vulnerabilities.

item.title: Britain seeks views before it drops the hammer on signal jammers
item.description: The UK government is gathering public opinions on radiofrequency jammers as it prepares to legislate against these devices, which are often linked to criminal activities. This consultation period lasts four weeks.

item.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman's tumultuous leadership at OpenAI is explored in a recent article, detailing his brief firing and subsequent reinstatement, along with his efforts to reshape the organization. This period has raised questions about leadership stability in AI companies.

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: OpenAI has supported an Illinois bill that would limit liability for AI companies in cases of mass harm caused by their products. This controversial stance raises ethical questions about accountability in AI development.

item.title: ACM Human-Computer Interaction Conference (CHI) 2026
item.description: Apple is set to showcase new research at the ACM CHI Conference 2026 in Barcelona, focusing on human-computer interaction. This event gathers experts from both scientific and industrial backgrounds.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new feature allowing organization admins to access Copilot directly from security assessment results, providing contextual guidance for risk management. This aims to enhance security processes.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to automate ongoing business tasks using AI agents. This development aims to simplify operations for organizations looking to leverage AI.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta AI's app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store following the launch of its new model, Muse Spark, indicating a significant increase in user interest and engagement.

item.title: Microsoft named a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Sovereign Cloud Platforms
item.description: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Sovereign Cloud Platforms, reflecting its strong position in addressing evolving sovereignty needs in cloud services.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has limited the release of its Mythos model, citing concerns over its ability to identify security exploits in widely used software. This raises questions about the balance between innovation and safety in AI development.
